zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche於1224年出生在威爾士福林爾曼諾 安德 拉可時，她的父親Sir Thomas II Le Waleys, Baron Waleys of Pembroke31歲，母親Matilda30歲。她於1245年與John De La Roche在英國威爾斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1315年在威爾士布洛可爾羅克 卡素享年91歲，埋葬在威爾士布洛可爾羅克 卡素。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche于1224年出生在威尔士福林爾曼諾 安德 拉可时，她的父亲Sir Thomas II Le Waleys, Baron Waleys of Pembroke31岁，母亲Matilda30岁。她于1245年与John De La Roche在英国威尔斯结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1315年在威尔士布洛可爾羅克 卡素享年91岁，埋葬在威尔士布洛可爾羅克 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: Waleys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

habitational name from Wales near Sheffield (South Yorkshire). The placename means ‘The Welshmen’ and denoted a settlement of Brittonic speakers living on the boundary between the Anglo-Saxon kingdoms of Northumbria and Mercia (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). See also Wallis .
habitational name from Wales, the country named from Old English wēalas ‘foreigners’ (especially those speaking Celtic or Romance languages). This meaning of the surname is quite rare.

variants.name: Wailes
meanings: English: variant of   Wales  .

variants.name: Wallis
meanings: Scottish and English: from Anglo-Norman French Waleis, Walais ‘Welshman’, also sometimes ‘Breton’ (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning a ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). In western and central England the medieval reference is clearly to Welshmen. In Norfolk and Lincolnshire, the reference was probably to Bretons, many of whom settled in the eastern counties after the Conquest. Compare Welsh , Walsh . The idea that the Scottish surname refers to the Welsh-speaking Britons of Strathclyde is erroneous.

nameMeanings.name: Baroness
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Nels.

Norwegian: old variant of Barsnes (and, in North America, probably also an altered form of this), a habitational name from a farmstead so named in the county of Sogn og Fjordane, possibly from a compound of the Old Norse personal name Baldrekr + nes ‘point, headland’.

nameMeanings.name: Roche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aidan, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, Fionnuala, Kevin Patrick, Liam.
Irish (Munster) and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Roach  . Compare   Roch  .

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a rocky outcrop or crag, from Old French roche ‘rock’. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the variant Laroche 2. Compare Deroche .

variants.name: Laroche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Normand, Pierre, Jacques, Laurent, Adrien, Cecile, Germain, Marcel.
French:
habitational name from any of numerous places in various parts of France named Laroche or La Roche, from Old French roche ‘rock’, with feminine definite article la.

variants.name: Larocque
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Henri, Marcel, Normand, Valmore, Yves, Achille, Amie, Donat, Emile.

French: habitational name from La Ro(c)que, the name of several places in various parts of France, from a regional variant of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche , compare Laroche ), with feminine definite article la. Compare Laroque .

Altered form of southern French Roquebrune or of its variant Rocquebrune: habitational name from Ro(c)quebrune, the name of several places in the southern part of France. The surname Roquebrune is rare in France, while the variant Rocquebrune is extinct there. Compare Larock , Lerocque , Rockburn , and Rocque 1.

variants.name: Rochet
meanings: French: diminutive of   Roche  , a topographic name meaning ‘rock’, or a habitational name from (Le) Rochet, the name of several places in various parts of France.
Catalan: of French origin (see above). This surname is also found in the Dominican Republic and Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Rochat
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique, Philippe, Pierre.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French roche ‘rock’ (see Roche 2), or a habitational name from Le Rochat, the name of several places in the eastern part of France.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Roquet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Camille.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche and Roque ), or a habitational name from (Le) Roquet, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Rochez
meanings: Walloon and French: variant of   Roche   2 or   Rocher  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Profit’ or ‘sharp’.
2  ♀, ♂ ‘Upright’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche was born in 1224, in Manor and Rake, Flintshire, Wales, her father, Sir Thomas II Le Waleys, Baron Waleys of Pembroke, was 31 and her mother, Matilda, was 30. She married John De La Roche in 1245, in Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1315, in Roch Castle, Pembrokeshire, Wales, at the age of 91, and was buried in Roch Castle, Pembrokeshire, Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche est née en 1224, à Manor and Rake, Flintshire, Galles, son père, Sir Thomas II Le Waleys, Baron Waleys of Pembroke, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Matilda, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé John De La Roche en 1245, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1315, à Roch Castle, Pembrokeshire, Galles, à l’âge de 91 ans, et a été enterrée à Roch Castle, Pembrokeshire, Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche 1224 in Manor and Rake, Flintshire, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas II Le Waleys, Baron Waleys of Pembroke 31 und ihre Mutter Matilda 30. Sie hat 1245 in Wales, United Kingdom John De La Roche geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1315 im Alter von 91 Jahren in Roch Castle, Pembrokeshire, Wales und wurde in Roch Castle, Pembrokeshire, Wales begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Rocheが1224年にウェールズフリントシュアイアマノー アンド ラクで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas II Le Waleys, Baron Waleys of Pembrokeは31歳，母Matildaは30歳であった。1245年にイギリスウェールズでJohn De La Rocheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1315年にウェールズペンブロケシュアイアロチ カストルで91歳で死去し，ウェールズペンブロケシュアイアロチ カストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche(이)가 1224년 웨일스 플린츠히르 먼어 앤드 라크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas II Le Waleys, Baron Waleys of Pembroke(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1245년 영국 웨일스에서 John De La Roche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1315년 웨일스 p브로케실르 록h 카스틀에서 91세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스 p브로케실르 록h 카스틀에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche родилась в 1224 г. в местности манор анд реик, флинцхир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas II Le Waleys, Baron Waleys of Pembroke было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Matilda, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John De La Roche в 1245 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1315 г. в местности роч кастл, пемброкешир, Уэльс в возрасте 91 год и была похоронена в местности роч кастл, пемброкешир, Уэльс.
